commonIssues:
Problems with the Sarathi portal's 'Aadhaar-based' LL application, such as OTP not being received or face-authentication failing multiple times.

How to fix: Ensure your mobile number is updated in the Aadhaar database. If face-authentication fails, choose the 'Non-Aadhaar' route, which requires a physical visit to the RTO but avoids the digital mismatch issues.

Rajasthan has frequently faced delays in printing and dispatching DL smart cards due to tender issues for the cards themselves.

How to fix: Download the 'Virtual DL' from the mParivahan app. In 2021, the Rajasthan government officially declared that digital licenses are valid and police cannot demand a physical card if the digital one is shown via an authorized app.

Difficulty in renewing older licenses that were not entered into the national registry (Sarathi).

How to fix: Visit your local RTO with the original license and apply for 'Backlog Entry'. This process usually takes 7–10 days, after which your license will be available for online renewal or duplication.

Some E-Mitra centers charge more than the government-prescribed fees for applying for a DL or booking a slot.

How to fix: Check the official fee structure on the Rajasthan Transport Department website. Pay the fees directly on the Sarathi portal using your own debit/credit card to avoid being overcharged by intermediaries.
